sections.main_section: A) Compliance, Page Limit, and Reproducibility ( /12)

items.description: Adult task is correct (binary classification on income) - target and labels match spec, used consistently in plots/metrics
Wine task is correct (multiclass classification on quality) - quality treated as discrete classes, Macro-F1 reported

items: Wrong target for Adult, different dataset, or unclear label mapping
Wine treated as regression or binarized without authorization

items.description: Runs DT, kNN, SVM (at least two kernels), and NN (SGD only; scikit-learn and PyTorch) on both datasets

items: Any algorithm family missing or only run on one dataset

subsections.subsection: A3. Submission integrity and page limit compliance ( /2)

items.description: Overleaf project link is included and is read-only
Report complies with the page limit and required content is within the graded portion

items: Missing, broken, or requires edit permissions for Overleaf link
Exceeds page limit or required content beyond what will be graded

items.description: Git commit hash is provided and repository is accessible to course staff
Run instructions are complete enough to reproduce outputs on a standard Linux machine (clear commands, environment requirements, seeds provided)

items: Missing, invalid, or inaccessible git hash
Instructions missing or clearly insufficient to reproduce results

items.description: EDA includes core facts for both datasets: shape, feature types, missingness, and class counts; supports later choices (metrics, preprocessing, model expectations)

items: EDA is absent, superficial, or incorrect

subsections.subsection: B2. Hypotheses stated early and grounded ( /5)

items.description: At least one hypothesis per dataset stated after EDA and before tuned results; predicts algorithm performance/trade-offs and is testable

items: Hypothesis is just a goal or stated after results

items.description: Hypotheses revisited and evaluated using reported results (LC, MC, metrics); connects outcomes back to hypothesis and explains surprises

items: Hypotheses never revisited or ignored in discussion

sections.main_section: C) Evaluation Design, Cross-Validation, and Sanity ( /15)

subsections.subsection: C1. Train, validation, and test separation ( /6)

items.description: Test set is held out and used only once for final reporting; split explicit and test set not used for tuning
Hyperparameter tuning uses training-only validation (cross-validation or fixed validation split); tuning method and data used clearly stated

items: Hyperparameters chosen based on test performance
Validation unclear or appears to include test data

items.description: Validation procedure appropriate and reproducible; reports split strategy (fold count or train/val ratio), stratification, and aggregation method

items: Validation referenced without enough detail to verify (e.g., 'we used CV' only)

subsections.subsection: C3. Sanity checks and suspicious results ( /5)

items.description: Includes at least one baseline or sanity check and investigates unusually strong results; baseline (e.g., majority-class) or Leakage/Evaluation Debug subsection when warranted

items: No sanity checks reported and near-perfect metrics not questioned

items.description: Uses at least one complexity control (max_depth, min_samples_leaf, ccp_alpha); explicitly identifies control and why it prevents overfitting
Includes DT learning curve (train and validation vs training size) with interpretation
Includes DT model-complexity curve over pruning/regularization parameter with interpretation
Reports Adult and Wine final test metrics and confusion matrices for DT
Reports DT runtime (fit and predict) and notes at least one trade-off
DT hyperparameter exploration meaningful (multiple values tested, final choice justified by MC curve evidence)

items: No complexity control used
Missing learning curve or model-complexity curve
Missing final metrics or confusion matrices
No runtime reporting or trade-off discussion
Hyperparameter exploration insufficient or not justified

items.description: Tests meaningfully different k values and justifies the chosen region
Includes kNN learning curve with interpretation
Includes kNN model-complexity curve (validation vs k) with interpretation
Reports Adult and Wine final test metrics and confusion matrices for kNN
Reports kNN runtime (fit and predict) and discusses predict-time cost
kNN hyperparameter exploration meaningful (multiple k values tested, final k justified by MC evidence)

items: Insufficient exploration of k values
Missing learning curve or model-complexity curve
Missing final metrics or confusion matrices
No runtime reporting or predict-time cost discussion
Hyperparameter exploration insufficient or not justified

items.description: Uses at least two kernels per dataset (linear and RBF minimum)
Includes SVM learning curve with interpretation
Includes SVM model-complexity curve (vary C and γ) with interpretation
Reports Adult and Wine final test metrics and confusion matrices for SVM
Reports SVM runtime (fit and predict) and notes kernel trade-offs
SVM hyperparameter exploration meaningful and interpretable for both kernels; explores C (and γ for RBF) with enough values to support justified conclusion

items: Less than two kernels used per dataset
Missing learning curve or model-complexity curve
Missing final metrics or confusion matrices
No runtime reporting or kernel trade-off discussion
Hyperparameter exploration insufficient for one/both kernels

sections.main_section: G) Neural Networks (SGD-only; scikit-learn and PyTorch) ( /14)

items.description: SGD-only rule followed; training setup documented (learning rate, batch size, epochs, regularization); optimizer explicitly SGD, no disallowed methods
Includes epoch curve (train and validation loss or metric) with interpretation
Includes NN learning curve with interpretation
Includes NN model-complexity curve varying one key hyperparameter with interpretation
Reports Adult and Wine final NN test metrics and confusion matrices
Required scikit-learn versus PyTorch NN comparison present for each dataset with fair interpretation
NN hyperparameter exploration meaningful (multiple values tested, final choice justified with MC and epoch evidence)

items: SGD-only rule violated or setup undocumented
Missing epoch curve, learning curve, or model-complexity curve
Missing final metrics or confusion matrices
Missing sklearn vs PyTorch comparison
Hyperparameter exploration insufficient or not justified

items.description: Cross-model synthesis compares algorithms across both datasets using evidence-backed takeaways
Writing interprets figures (why it happened), not only reporting numbers
Includes at least two peer-reviewed sources and includes AI/LLM disclosure if used

items: Weak or missing cross-dataset algorithm comparison
Writing style reports numbers without interpretation
Insufficient peer-reviewed sources or missing AI disclosure

sections.main_section: Extra Credit: Activation Function Study (up to 5)

items.description: Evaluates four activation functions (ReLU, GELU, SiLU/Swish, tanh) using same architecture, preprocessing, regularization, training protocol; SGD-only, one dataset
Includes epoch-wise validation curves for all four activations (same training budget)
Includes final comparison table of metrics (runtime and convergence notes)
Provides concise interpretation of convergence stability and trade-offs under SGD

items: Missing activations / uncontrolled changes (LR/init/reg) / incomplete curves or metrics
Descriptive commentary without diagnosing stability or trade-offs

verification_notes.section: Verification Step (TA spot-check; not always required)
verification_notes.content:
TAs may perform brief verification when: (1) results appear suspiciously strong, (2) methodology unclear, or (3) reproducibility claims inconsistent.
- GitHub verification: Confirm commit hash exists and corresponds to submitted results/figures.
- Overleaf verification: Confirm read-only Overleaf link exists with reasonable revision history.
If submission fails verification materially, escalate to Head TA.
